steps.title: Identify the initial value from the table
steps.content:
At $t = 0$, the amount is $n = 867.00$. This is the initial coefficient in exponential growth.
Initial value: $a = 867$

steps.title: Calculate the growth rate using consecutive data points
steps.content:
The growth factor is the ratio between consecutive years. From $t = 0$ to $t = 1$:
$$\frac{870.47}{867} \approx 1.004$$
Since the growth factor equals $(1 + r)$, we have $1 + r = 1.004$, so $r = 0.004$.

steps.title: Write the exponential equation
steps.content:
With initial value $a = 867$ and rate $r = 0.004$, the equation is:
$$n = 867(1 + 0.004)^t$$
Verify at $t = 2$: $n = 867(1.004)^2 = 867 \times 1.00800160 \approx 873.95$ ✓

That matches choice B — B is correct.
